title: Runtime Monitor
description: Real-time hardware resource usage and API request statistics
runtime_summary: How long the service has been running
request_chart_description: Track request and error trends to understand peak and quiet periods.
latency_chart_description: Spot when average latency lifts and user experience may degrade.
process_memory_summary: Current RSS footprint of the service
process_cpu_summary: Best quick signal for live service pressure
success_rate_summary: Success rate is most useful when read with tail latency
status_chart_description: Track the balance of success, client errors, and server errors over time
top_slow_description: Start with the endpoints that have the worst average latency
top_error_description: Start with the endpoints generating the most failures
redis_hit_rate_summary: Higher hit rate usually means more stable cache payoff
redis_commands_hint: Total commands processed since Redis started
db_waiting_hint: Left is waiting sessions, right is blocked queries
db_longest_query_hint: Only active client queries in the current database
db_size_hint: Includes tables, indexes, toast, and free space
db_top_queries_description: Use this to spot slow SQL and hot SQL quickly
db_top_queries_disabled: When the extension is unavailable, the reason is shown here
db_pgss_not_installed: pg_stat_statements is not installed, so Top SQL is unavailable.
db_pgss_not_loaded: pg_stat_statements is not loaded via shared_preload_libraries, so Top SQL is unavailable.
db_pgss_permission_denied: The current database role cannot read pg_stat_statements.
db_pgss_unsupported_version: The current PostgreSQL version exposes incompatible pg_stat_statements columns.
db_pgss_query_failed: Failed to read pg_stat_statements, so Top SQL is unavailable.
overview_requests_hint: A fast read on overall traffic during the monitoring window
overview_errors_hint: A quick signal for stability pressure in the current window
overview_latency_hint: Useful for spotting broad response-time degradation first
overview_runtime_hint: Judge overall load with process CPU and DB cache efficiency together
group_system_description: Host-level resources and network throughput for environment pressure checks.
group_runtime_description: Focus on memory and CPU pressure inside the current service process.
group_database_description: Connections, cache, transactions, and SQL hotspots in one place.
group_redis_description: Cache hit rate, connections, and memory to judge cache health quickly.
group_performance_description: Use success rate and tail latency to judge API experience stability.
group_business_description: Use business entity scale to understand current system footprint.
group_charts_description: Keep request, latency, and status trends in the visual center first.
group_endpoints_description: Use slowest and noisiest endpoints to focus investigation quickly.
load_avg_hint: Above CPU core count means queue is forming
group_gpu_description: GPU device utilization, memory, temperature, and power. Shows unavailable when no GPU is detected.
gpu_no_gpu_hint: No NVIDIA GPU detected on this host, or nvidia-smi is not available
cpu_cores_chart_description: Compare real-time load across all cores to spot hot cores instantly.
net_chart_description: Total bytes received vs. sent since boot — shows traffic direction balance.
db_rows_chart_description: Read vs. write operation breakdown to characterize database workload type.
redis_hit_chart_description: Hit vs. miss ratio to judge cache efficiency at a glance.
disk_io_speed_hint: Read/write rate in the last 1 second
gpu_chart_description: Compare compute utilization and memory usage across all GPU devices.
